goal: 扩大 cxs skill 对本机配置考古问题的触发范围

summary.title: Expand cxs skill triggers for local setup archaeology
summary.what: Updated the published cxs skill and local cxsd dogfood skill wording so prompts about this Mac's configured servers, VPS nodes, services, domains, providers, or past setup work are routed to cxs even when the user does not explicitly say cxs or history.
summary.why: The existing cxs description over-indexed on explicit prior-session words such as previous, last time, or history. User prompts like '本机有哪几台服务器的配置，都配过些啥服务' express setup archaeology but did not reliably trigger the skill.
summary.user_goal: 扩大 cxs skill 对本机配置考古问题的触发范围

decisions.chose: Put the setup-archaeology trigger language in YAML description.
decisions.rationale: Skill metadata is the primary trigger surface; body-only guidance would not fix under-triggering.
rejected: Only adding examples inside the skill body

decisions.chose: Add the concrete user example and related Chinese trigger phrases such as 本机有哪些服务器配置, 都配过啥服务, 配过, 配置过, 装过, 调过.
decisions.rationale: The failing class is expressed as local setup inventory rather than explicit history search.

decisions.chose: Describe cxs as historical evidence to use before or alongside memory/live inspection.
decisions.rationale: cxs should surface prior session evidence without replacing runtime/config verification.

decisions.chose: Mirror the scenario into cxsd while preserving the cxs vs cxsd release/dev split.
decisions.rationale: Dogfood should exercise the same trigger concept but still route unpublished checks to cxsd.

rejected.alternative: Add a new CLI command or retrieval behavior
rejected.reason: The issue is skill routing, not query semantics.

rejected.alternative: Install dirty local cxs skill over the published global cxs copy
rejected.reason: Published cxs should update through the repo/install flow rather than a local dirty copy.

risks: The broader trigger wording may cause cxs to be invoked for some live-state inventory questions; the skill body mitigates this by requiring live verification when the prompt lacks historical setup semantics.

anti_patterns.what: Only trigger cxs when the user explicitly says history, previous session, or cxs
anti_patterns.why: Many setup-archaeology questions are phrased as current inventory but require prior Codex session evidence to reconstruct.

followups: After pushing, refresh the installed global cxs skill with npx skills add catoncat/cxs --full-depth --skill cxs -g -a codex -y and restart Codex to load the new metadata.

architectural_claims: Skill trigger behavior is driven primarily by the SKILL.md frontmatter description.
behavioral_changes: Agents should now select cxs for local setup archaeology prompts about configured servers, VPS nodes, services, domains, providers, and similar past setup work.
migration_notes: No CLI or index migration required; refresh installed skill metadata after repo update.
